name: The Drovers' Roads Trail
name_original: Los Caminos de la Trashumancia – trail running circular desde Villayuste (Reserva de la Biosfera Valles de Omaña y Luna)
description: This difficult 9.7-mile trail running route from Villayuste explores the historic Paths of Transhumance in the Valles de Omaña y Luna Biosph
region_intro: Jogging routes around Soto Y Amío traverse a diverse landscape within the Valles de Omaña y Luna Biosphere Reserve, characterized by mountains, fertile valleys, and narrow passages shaped by the Luna and Omaña rivers. The region features extensive forests, including ancient oak and birch groves, providing shaded paths. Joggers can explore varied terrain, from riverbanks to high mountain landscapes, with elevations reaching over 2,000 meters in the Sierra de la Cañada. Best jogging routes around Soto Y Amío The most popular jogging route is Ruta de los Calderones Trail, a 3.7 miles (6.0 km) trail that takes about 45 minutes to complete, leading through the geological enclave of Los Calderones Gorge. Another top favourite among local runners is The Drovers' Roads Trail, a difficult 9.7 miles (15.7 km) path that explores historic transhumance routes with significant elevation gain. Local runners also love the Cordel de Merinas Trail, a 5.7 miles (9.1 km) trail leading through wooded areas and along the historic Cordel de Merinas, often completed in about 1 hour 7 minutes. Jogging around Soto Y Amío is defined by its mountains, river valleys, and extensive forests, offering a mix of natural beauty and varied terrain. The network provides options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging routes. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 50 runners have used komoot to explore Soto Y Amío's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Soto y Amío?
A: There are &lt;strong&gt;8&lt;/strong&gt; dedicated running routes around Soto y Amío listed on komoot, offering a variety of experiences within the Valles de Omaña y Luna Biosphere Reserve.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails in Soto y Amío?
A: The running trails in Soto y Amío feature a diverse landscape, from fertile valleys and riverbanks along the Luna and Omaña rivers to more challenging high mountain paths. You'll encounter extensive forests, including ancient oak and birch groves, providing shaded sections, as well as open areas with panoramic views, especially towards peaks over 2,000 meters in the Sierra de la Cañada.

Q: Are there running routes suitable for all fitness levels?
A: Yes, Soto y Amío offers a range of running routes. While there are no routes classified as 'easy' in this guide, you'll find &lt;strong&gt;3 moderate&lt;/strong&gt; routes and &lt;strong&gt;5 difficult&lt;/strong&gt; routes, ensuring options for both intermediate runners and those seeking a more challenging experience with significant elevation changes.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in the area?
A: Yes, several routes in Soto y Amío are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1055974406/la-tierra-de-ordas-running-circular-desde-santa-maria-de-ordas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Tierra de Ordás – running circular desde Santa María de Ordás&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 12 km loop, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31455045" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz de Fontañán loop from Olleros de Alba&lt;/a&gt; offers a challenging 11.8 km circular run.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by waterfalls?
A: Absolutely! The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1052018123/ruta-de-los-calderones-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de los Calderones Trail&lt;/a&gt; leads through the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4073598" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Calderones Gorge&lt;/a&gt;, where you can discover multiple waterfalls, including the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5558248" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall of the Arroyo de los Calderones&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural attractions can I see while running in Soto y Amío?
A: Many running routes offer views of the region's natural beauty. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4073598" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Calderones Gorge&lt;/a&gt;, pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5548992" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barrios de Luna Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, or run through the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5741568" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hayedo de la Boyariza&lt;/a&gt; forest. The region's diverse landscapes, from river valleys to high mountains, are a constant highlight.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks along the running paths?
A: Yes, some routes offer a glimpse into the region's history. For instance, the area around &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5556607" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Fontañán Summit&lt;/a&gt; features remnants from the Spanish Civil War, such as trenches and tunnels. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5558242" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Virgin of Manadero in the Cave of the Doves&lt;/a&gt;, a unique religious building nestled in the landscape.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Soto y Amío?
A: Over 50 runners have used komoot to explore Soto y Amío's varied terrain. They often praise the region for its natural beauty, diverse landscapes, and the mix of challenging and moderate paths. The extensive forests and river valleys are frequently highlighted as enjoyable features for running.

Q: Is parking available near the running trailheads?
A: While specific parking details for each trailhead are best checked on individual route pages, Soto y Amío is generally accessible by car. Many villages and starting points for trails, especially those popular with hikers and runners, typically offer parking options. It's advisable to check the komoot route details for the most precise information on parking at your chosen starting point.

Q: What is the best season for running in Soto y Amío?
A: Soto y Amío offers good running conditions throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, with autumn showcasing beautiful fall foliage in the extensive forests. Summer can be warm, but the shaded paths through oak and birch groves, as well as riparian forests along riverbanks, offer cooler options. Winter running is possible, especially on lower elevation trails, but higher mountain areas may require appropriate gear for snow and ice.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the running trails in Soto y Amío?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on trails in natural areas like Soto y Amío, especially within the Valles de Omaña y Luna Biosphere Reserve. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure the comfort of other trail users. Be mindful of any specific local signage or regulations, particularly in protected areas or near livestock.

Q: Are there running trails suitable for families?
A: While this guide focuses on moderate to difficult routes, the region does offer easier paths suitable for families. The 'Senda de Matacorral' is mentioned as an easy and short route in the region, perfect for a family outing. When choosing a route, consider the distance and elevation gain to match your family's fitness level. The varied terrain ensures there are options for different abilities.
tips: We are in the Alto del Camparón (passage area of the Los Caminos de la Transhumancia route) with beautiful views of the Omañeses mountains and to the north, the Luna mountain.
From Alto 'El Viso', we have a magnificent panoramic view of the Omaña mountains dominated by large expanses of oak groves.

Without a doubt, the king of the Oman mountains is the oak and here we find ourselves under the shade of a beautiful oak grove. A transit area of the route of Los Caminos de la Transhumancia that starts from Villayuste.

faqs.question: Where does the trail for Los Caminos de la Trashumancia start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route that starts and ends in Villayuste. While specific parking areas aren't detailed, Villayuste is a small town, and you should be able to find roadside parking or a designated area near the village center to begin your run.

faqs.question: Is Los Caminos de la Trashumancia suitable for beginner trail runners?
faqs.answer: This trail is generally considered difficult for trail running by Komoot and requires a medium-high level of physical preparation. While some sources describe it as having low difficulty for hiking, the varied terrain and approximately 16 kilometers length make it challenging for jogging. It's not ideal for beginners.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this trail running route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied. You'll start with an ascent through farmlands and fallow grounds, leading to Las Eras de Redimil. It then transitions into oak woodlands, degraded oak forests, broom, and heather. You'll encounter old paths, traditional 'cordeles' (cattle tracks), and a small valley with old water mills. Be aware that some sections might have less distinct signage.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route of Los Caminos de la Trashumancia?
faqs.answer: The route offers picturesque views of the Omaña region and the Luna mountains. You'll pass through Villayuste and the small town of Lago, which features traditional Omañesa architecture. Highlights include the Alto del Camparón and a View from Alto El Viso, both offering great vistas. You'll also traverse an Oak Grove Trail and a valley with ancient water mills.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to run this trail?
faqs.answer: The Valles de Omaña y Luna Biosphere Reserve offers beautiful scenery year-round, but for trail running, spring and autumn are often ideal. The weather is typically milder, and the natural landscapes are vibrant. Summer can be hot, and winter might bring snow or colder conditions, making some sections more challenging.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on Los Caminos de la Trashumancia trail?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for dogs aren't provided, it's common in rural areas like this for dogs to be allowed, often on a leash. However, be aware that you might encounter sheep flocks, which are frequently guarded by mastiffs. Exercise caution and keep your dog under strict control to ensure safety for everyone.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or is there an entrance fee to access the Valles de Omaña y Luna Biosphere Reserve?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no entrance fees or special permits required to access the Valles de Omaña y Luna Biosphere Reserve for activities like trail running. However, always respect local regulations, stay on marked paths, and practice Leave No Trace principles to preserve the natural environment.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route partially overlaps with other established trails. Approximately 28% of the route follows sections of the Ruta de las Cuencas Mineras del Carbón, and about 24% coincides with parts of the Camino Olvidado (La Robla-Cirujales).

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trail?
faqs.answer: As the trail is within the Valles de Omaña y Luna Biosphere Reserve, you might encounter various wildlife. Notably, you are likely to see sheep flocks, often accompanied by mastiffs, which are working dogs. Keep a respectful distance from livestock and their guardians.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes, restaurants, or accommodation options near Villayuste?
faqs.answer: Villayuste is a small, traditional village. While specific establishments aren't listed, you might find a local bar or guesthouse. For more extensive services, including cafes, restaurants, and accommodation, you may need to venture to larger towns within the Omaña y Luna region.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a trail run on Los Caminos de la Trashumancia?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and varied terrain, it's advisable to bring appropriate trail running shoes, sufficient water, snacks, and a fully charged phone. Due to potentially less distinct signage in some parts, a GPS device or the Komoot app with the downloaded route is highly recommended for navigation. Layers of clothing are also wise, as weather conditions can change.
